title: Input Actions, Bindings, Focus, and Keyboard Equivalence · Runtime Lens: Input Equivalence
summary: Canvas clicks are the only navigation method, global key bindings fire inside text fields, focus becomes invisible, and repeat events race state changes. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the input equivalence increment in remappable action and focus-navigation map. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Give pointer-only operations keyboard or single-action alternatives without imposing holds, repeats, or precise timing.
Provide logical focus order, programmatic focus placement, visible focus, escape paths, and focus restoration after dialogs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: maze-input-equivalence — Give pointer-only operations keyboard or single-action alternatives without imposing holds, repeats, or precise timing.
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-input-focus-bindings-2
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-input-focus-bindings-2.
# Case 7417: a keyboard-only puzzle tournament.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant.
def worked_maze_input_focus_bindings_2(actions: int = 9, keyboard_equivalents: int = 9, focus_stops: int = 7, trapped_stops: int = 0, ambiguous_bindings: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "gridworked2",
        "topology7417",
        "search2dekrow",
        "eventworked27417",
        "access7417worked2",
        "recovery2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if actions < 1 or keyboard_equivalents < 0:
        return False, keyboard_equivalents, "action-count"
    if keyboard_equivalents != actions:
        return False, keyboard_equivalents, "input-equivalence"
    if focus_stops < 1 or trapped_stops > 0:
        return False, trapped_stops, "focus-order"
    if ambiguous_bindings > 0:
        return False, ambiguous_bindings, "binding-scope"
    assert keyboard_equivalents == actions
    return True, keyboard_equivalents, "equivalent-input-map"

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-input-equivalence-1byvcr
# Competency: maze-input-equivalence.
# Case 2592: a low-vision player session.
# Operating constraint: resource use must remain below an explicit budget.
# Changed case: inject one invalid wall, stale callback, or corrupted field and reject it.
def evidence_maze_input_equivalence_1byvcr(actions: int = 9, keyboard_equivalents: int = 9, focus_stops: int = 7, trapped_stops: int = 0, ambiguous_bindings: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1byvcr = "1byvcr-rcvyb1-2592"
    assert evidence_variant_1byvcr
    evidence_axes_1byvcr = (
        "grid1byvcr",
        "topology2592",
        "searchrcvyb1",
        "event1byvcr2592",
        "access25921byvcr",
        "recoveryrcvyb12592",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1byvcr)) == 6
    if actions < 1 or keyboard_equivalents < 0:
        return False, keyboard_equivalents, "action-count"
    if keyboard_equivalents != actions:
        return False, keyboard_equivalents, "input-equivalence"
    if focus_stops < 1 or trapped_stops > 0:
        return False, trapped_stops, "focus-order"
    if ambiguous_bindings > 0:
        return False, ambiguous_bindings, "binding-scope"
    assert keyboard_equivalents == actions
    return True, keyboard_equivalents, "equivalent-input-map"

steps.why: Retrieving Input Equivalence through the concrete remappable action and focus-navigation map case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Input Equivalence model · Theory Input Equivalence, keep Input Equivalence connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Give pointer-only operations keyboard or single-action alternatives without imposing holds, repeats, or precise timing.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Input Equivalence model · Theory Input Equivalence, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Input Equivalence precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Input Equivalence model · Theory Input Equivalence, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 3215 during a classroom algorithm comparison. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile named actions, keyboard equivalence, focus order, and binding scope; native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution; then inject one invalid wall, stale callback, or corrupted field and reject it. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Give pointer-only operations keyboard or single-action alternatives without imposing holds, repeats, or precise timing.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Input Equivalence model · Theory Input Equivalence, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: A clickable cell is accessible because every desktop has a mouse.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: maze-input-equivalence-1byvcr(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_maze_input_equivalence_1byvcr\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?actions < 1 or keyboard_equivalents < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?keyboard_equivalents != actions)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?focus_stops < 1 or trapped_stops > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?ambiguous_bindings > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
